title: Correct: checked_mul for K/M/G multipliers returns clean error
description: WHY THIS IS CORRECT: The lexer parses the numeric base with str::parse::<u64>() (which returns Err on overflow), then multiplies by the size suffix using checked_mul. Both overflow paths return a clean ParseError with the source location. A value like 18446744073709551616K gets rejected at the base parse step; a value like 18000000000G gets rejected at the checked_mul step. No silent wrapping or panic is possible on any input.
